findings.message: Adding "email" to frontend_rum_allowed_query_params captures the user's email address from the query string into RUM telemetry. This allowlist governs which query-string params are collected for attribution (the rest are UTM tracking params); an email is PII and is not needed for the stated attribution purpose. Capturing it into telemetry/traces is unnecessary PII collection and risks PII landing in observability backends and logs with no retention bound.
findings.suggestion: Remove the "email" entry from frontend_rum_allowed_query_params so the fail-closed allowlist keeps only the non-PII UTM attribution params. If a user identifier is genuinely required for analytics, use a pseudonymous/hashed id collected through a consented path rather than the raw email from the query string.
